Exercise 1 Sales Data Analysis (2015–2020) 1.1 Dataset 1.2 : Data Entry & Formulas 1.3 : Chart Creation 1.4 : Chart Formatting 1.5 : Interpretation 1.6 : Sorting 1.7 : Advanced Filter 1.8 Final Output 1.9 Conclusion

Exercise 1 Sales Data Analysis (2015–2020) Learning Outcomes - Analyse multi-region sales data - Apply SUM, AVERAGE, and Growth formulas - Create charts for comparison and trends - Perform sorting and filtering 1.1 Dataset Region Year Sales North 2015 42000 North 2016 46800 North 2017 51200 North 2018 55600 North 2019 60100 North 2020 65400 South 2015 35000 South 2016 38500 South 2017 41200 South 2018 44800 South 2019 48500 South 2020 53200 East 2015 28000 East 2016 31200 East 2017 35600 East 2018 39400 East 2019 43800 East 2020 49100 West 2015 32000 West 2016 35800 West 2017 40100 West 2018 44200 West 2019 49600 West 2020 55300  Download Excel File Download Sales Data Excel 1.2 : Data Entry & Formulas 1.2.1 Structure in Excel Column Description A Region B–G Sales (2015–2020) H Total I Average J Growth % 1.2.2 Formulas Used Calculation Formula Total =SUM(B4:G4) Average =AVERAGE(B4:G4) Growth % =(G4-B4)/B4 1.2.3 Grand Total Row Calculation Formula Year-wise Total =SUM(B4:B7) Overall Total =SUM(H4:H7) Avg of Avg =AVERAGE(I4:I7) 1.3 : Chart Creation 1.3.1 Recommended Charts Line Chart → Trend over years Column Chart → Region comparison 1.3.2 Diagram flowchart TD A[Select Data] --> B[Insert Tab] B --> C[Choose Chart] 1.3.3 Insight North region has highest sales All regions show upward growth trend 1.4 : Chart Formatting Title: Regional Sales Trend (2015–2020) X-axis: Year Y-axis: Sales Legend: Regions 1.5 : Interpretation Sales across all regions show steady growth. North region leads consistently, while East shows gradual improvement. Overall performance indicates positive business expansion. 1.6 : Sorting 1.6.1 Example Sort by 2020 Sales → Identify top region Sort by Growth % → Identify fastest growing region 1.7 : Advanced Filter 1.7.1 Example Criteria Region North OR Growth % >0.5 1.8 Final Output Multi-region Excel analysis Charts showing trends and comparison Summary interpretation 1.9 Conclusion This experiment demonstrates how Excel tools can be used effectively for business data analysis, visualization, and decision-making.

Exercise 2 Financial Data Analysis & Reporting 2.1 Dataset 2.2 Step 1: Data Entry & Formulas 2.3 Step 2: Chart Creation 2.4 Step 3: Chart Formatting 2.5 Step 4: Interpretation 2.6 Step 5: Sorting 2.7 Step 6: Advanced Filter 2.8 Step 7: Conditional Formatting 2.9 Step 8: Summary Statistics 2.10 Final Output 2.11 Conclusion

Exercise 2 Financial Data Analysis & Reporting Learning Outcomes - Analyse company financial performance - Apply ranking, logical, and statistical formulas - Perform filtering and risk analysis - Interpret financial indicators for decision-making 2.1 Dataset Company Revenue (Cr) Profit (Cr) EPS (Rs) ROE (%) Debt/Equity Reliance Industries 845000 75000 110 18 0.8 Tata Steel 150500 12800 42 14 1.2 HDFC Bank 160000 35000 60 20 0.5 Infosys 150000 32000 55 23 0.3 ITC 58500 15200 39 18 0.4  Download Excel File Download Financial Data Excel 2.2 Step 1: Data Entry & Formulas 2.2.1 Additional Columns in Excel Column Purpose Formula Rank by Profit Profit ranking =RANK(C4,C$4:C$8,0) Rank by ROE ROE ranking =RANK(E4,E$4:E$8,0) Risk Level Categorisation =IF(F4>1,“High Risk”,IF(F4>0.6,“Medium”,“Low Risk”)) Filter Result Condition check =IF(AND(E4>0.15,F4<1),“Qualifies”,“Excluded”) 2.3 Step 2: Chart Creation 2.3.1 Recommended Charts Column Chart → Compare Profit & Revenue Bar Chart → Compare ROE across companies flowchart TD A[Select Data] --> B[Insert Tab] B --> C[Choose Chart] 2.3.2 Insight Reliance has highest revenue and profit Infosys has highest ROE 2.4 Step 3: Chart Formatting Title: Company Financial Comparison X-axis: Company Y-axis: Values Legend: Metrics 2.5 Step 4: Interpretation Reliance Industries dominates in revenue and profit. Infosys shows strong efficiency with highest ROE. Tata Steel has higher financial risk due to high debt-equity ratio. 2.6 Step 5: Sorting 2.6.1 Example Sort by Profit → Identify top company Sort by ROE → Identify best performing company 2.7 Step 6: Advanced Filter 2.7.1 Criteria ROE (%) Debt/Equity >15 <1 2.7.2 Output Reliance Industries HDFC Bank Infosys ITC 2.8 Step 7: Conditional Formatting Highlight Debt/Equity > 1 → Tata Steel (High Risk) 2.9 Step 8: Summary Statistics Metric Formula Highest Revenue =MAX(B4:B8) Highest Profit =MAX(C4:C8) Best EPS =MAX(D4:D8) Avg ROE =AVERAGE(E4:E8) Lowest D/E =MIN(F4:F8) Companies ROE>15% =COUNTIF(E4:E8,“>0.15”) 2.10 Final Output Financial dataset with rankings Risk classification Filtered company list Summary insights 2.11 Conclusion This experiment demonstrates advanced Excel techniques for financial analysis including ranking, logical evaluation, and performance comparison.
